title: HIT 2024 Fall Computer Networks Labs (lab1, lab2)
summary: Two independent Java/Maven labs implementing core networking concepts. Lab 1 builds an HTTP proxy server with access control, URL caching, and conditional-request support. Lab 2 implements Go-Back-N and Selective Repeat reliable data transfer protocols over UDP with sliding-window logic and binary packet serialization.
instructionPreview: This is an undergraduate computer-networks course project. The repository contains two Java/Maven labs and one C lab: Lab 1 implements an HTTP proxy server with access control, URL redirection, and caching; Lab 2 implements reliable data transfer protocols (Go-Back-N and Selective Repeat) over UDP using sliding windows; Lab 4 implements IP-level raw-socket forwarding in C.
instruction:
This is an undergraduate computer-networks course project. The repository contains two Java/Maven labs and one C lab: Lab 1 implements an HTTP proxy server with access control, URL redirection, and caching; Lab 2 implements reliable data transfer protocols (Go-Back-N and Selective Repeat) over UDP using sliding windows; Lab 4 implements IP-level raw-socket forwarding in C.

Your working directory is `/workspace`. The project code is already present.
You need to implement all the modules and features listed below so that
all tests pass.

## Modules to Implement

The following modules need to be implemented or substantially built out:

- **Site Blocking Filter** (`src/lab1/src/main/java/cn/edu/hit/filter/FilterManager.java`): Checks whether a requested hostname is present in the blocked-sites set. (~1 method to implement)
- **User Blocking Filter** (`src/lab1/src/main/java/cn/edu/hit/filter/FilterManager.java`): Checks whether a client IP is present in the blocked-users set. (~1 method to implement)
- **Redirect Filter** (`src/lab1/src/main/java/cn/edu/hit/filter/FilterManager.java`): Returns a configured redirect target for a hostname or `null` when none exists. (~1 method to implement)
- **HTTP Header Parsing** (`src/lab1/src/main/java/cn/edu/hit/utils/HttpUtils.java`): Reads raw bytes until the `\r\n\r\n` boundary and returns the complete header string. (~1 function to implement)
- **HTTP Fixed-Length Body Parsing** (`src/lab1/src/main/java/cn/edu/hit/utils/HttpUtils.java`): Reads exactly the declared body size for `Content-Length` requests and responses. (~1 helper to implement)
- **HTTP Chunked Body Parsing** (`src/lab1/src/main/java/cn/edu/hit/utils/HttpUtils.java`): Decodes hexadecimal chunk sizes, chunk payloads, and the terminal zero chunk. (~1 helper to implement)
- **HTTP Line Reader** (`src/lab1/src/main/java/cn/edu/hit/utils/HttpUtils.java`): Reads CRLF-delimited lines for chunked-transfer parsing. (~1 helper to implement)
- **HTTP Request Parsing** (`src/lab1/src/main/java/cn/edu/hit/utils/HttpUtils.java`): Parses method, URI, version, headers, and optional fixed-length request bodies into `HttpRequest`. (~1 function to implement)
- **HTTP Response Parsing** (`src/lab1/src/main/java/cn/edu/hit/utils/HttpUtils.java`): Parses version, status, headers, and fixed-length or chunked bodies into `HttpResponse`. (~1 function to implement)
- **HTTP Chunked Body Forwarding** (`src/lab1/src/main/java/cn/edu/hit/utils/HttpUtils.java`): Emits response payload bytes using chunked transfer coding. (~1 helper to implement)
- **HTTP Fixed-Length Body Forwarding** (`src/lab1/src/main/java/cn/edu/hit/utils/HttpUtils.java`): Streams raw body bytes for `Content-Length` transfers. (~1 helper to implement)
- **HTTP Request Forwarding** (`src/lab1/src/main/java/cn/edu/hit/utils/HttpUtils.java`): Writes request heads and request-body bytes to the upstream server stream. (~1 function to implement)
- **HTTP Response Forwarding** (`src/lab1/src/main/java/cn/edu/hit/utils/HttpUtils.java`): Writes response heads and response-body bytes to the client stream using the correct transfer mode. (~1 function to implement)
- **HTTP Server Connection** (`src/lab1/src/main/java/cn/edu/hit/utils/HttpUtils.java`): Connects to an upstream server socket with a configurable timeout. (~1 function to implement)
- **HTTP Conditional Header Injection** (`src/lab1/src/main/java/cn/edu/hit/utils/HttpUtils.java`): Adds an `If-Modified-Since` header derived from cached metadata. (~1 function to implement)
- **String & File Utilities** (`src/lab1/src/main/java/cn/edu/hit/utils/StrUtils.java`, `FileUtils.java`): String hashing (SHA-256), hex encoding, and Java object serialization/deserialization for the cache. (~2 files, ~4 functions to implement)
- **Cache Manager** (`src/lab1/src/main/java/cn/edu/hit/cache/CacheManager.java`): Disk-backed response cache for the proxy. Must hash request URIs into cache filenames, load serialized cached responses when present, and persist fresh responses for reuse. (~1 file, ~2 functions to implement)
- **Packet Serialization** (`src/lab2/src/main/java/cn/edu/hit/core/Packet.java`, `ACK.java`): Binary serialization of data packets (1028-byte fixed layout) and ACK packets (1-byte). (~2 files, ~5 functions to implement)
- **RDT Support Utilities** (`src/lab2/src/main/java/cn/edu/hit/utils/IOUtils.java`, `Timer.java`): Shared support code for the reliable-transfer labs: file byte loading/appending and a reusable timeout scheduler for retransmission callbacks. (~2 files, ~5 functions to implement)
- **Go-Back-N Protocol** (`src/lab2/src/main/java/cn/edu/hit/core/GBNSender.java`, `GBNReceiver.java`): Full GBN sender and receiver implementation: sliding window management, packet sending with simulated loss, ACK reception and processing, timeout-based retransmission of entire window, and cumulative ACK handling. (~2 files, ~8 functions to implement)
- **Selective Repeat Protocol** (`src/lab2/src/main/java/cn/edu/hit/core/SRSender.java`, `SRReceiver.java`): Full SR sender and receiver implementation: per-packet timers, selective ACK handling, buffered out-of-order delivery, window-based sequence number management, and individual packet retransmission on timeout. (~2 files, ~12 functions to implement)
## Test-Coupled Signatures

The following functions are called directly by the test suite. You must implement
 them with these exact signatures:

- `FilterManager.isSiteBlocked(String site) -> boolean` — checks if a site is in the blocked-sites set
- `FilterManager.isUserBlocked(String user) -> boolean` — checks if a user IP is in the blocked-users set
- `FilterManager.getRedirect(String site) -> String` — returns redirect target or null
- `HttpUtils.parseHttpHeader(InputStream in) -> String` — reads bytes until `

` and returns complete header string
- `HttpUtils.parseHttpRequest(InputStream clientIn) -> HttpRequest` — parses method, URI, version, headers, and optional body
- `HttpUtils.parseHttpResponse(InputStream serverIn) -> HttpResponse` — parses version, status code, headers, and body (fixed-length or chunked)
- `HttpUtils.forwardHttpRequest(HttpRequest request, OutputStream out) -> void` — forwards request heads and body bytes to an output stream
- `HttpUtils.forwardHttpResponse(HttpResponse response, OutputStream out) -> void` — forwards response heads and body bytes to an output stream
- `HttpUtils.connectToServer(String host, int port, int timeout) -> Socket` — opens an upstream TCP socket using the provided timeout
- `HttpUtils.addIfModifiedSinceHeader(HttpRequest request, ZonedDateTime lastModified) -> HttpRequest` — returns a request with an updated conditional header
- `CacheManager.get(String uri) -> HttpResponse` — loads a cached response for a URI or returns null when absent
- `CacheManager.put(String uri, HttpResponse response) -> void` — persists a response so later requests can reuse it
- `Packet.toBytes() -> byte[]` — serializes to exactly 1028 bytes
- `Packet.fromBytes(byte[] bytes) -> Packet` — deserializes from 1028-byte array
- `Packet.getData() -> byte[]` — returns actual data trimmed to dataLength
- `ACK.toBytes() -> byte[]` — serializes ACK to 1-byte array
- `ACK.fromBytes(byte[] bytes) -> ACK` — deserializes ACK from 1-byte array
- `IOUtils.readBytesFromFile(String fileName) -> byte[]` — reads the full file into memory
- `IOUtils.appendBytesToFile(String fileName, byte[] data) -> void` — appends delivered bytes to an output file
- `Timer.start(Runnable task) -> void` — schedules a timeout callback
- `Timer.stop() -> void` — cancels the current timer and allows restart
- `GBNSender.sendData(byte[] data) -> void` — GBN sender main loop with sliding window
- `GBNSender.windowSlide(int ackNum) -> int` — computes how many positions the window should slide
- `GBNReceiver.receiveData(String fileName) -> void` — GBN receiver loop writing in-order data to file

After implementing all functions, rebuild both Maven projects with `mvn package -DskipTests` in `src/lab1/` and `src/lab2/` respectively.

moduleDag.project: HIT 2024 Fall Computer Networks Labs (lab1, lab2)
moduleDag.description: Two independent Java/Maven labs implementing core networking concepts. Lab 1 builds an HTTP proxy server with access control, URL caching, and conditional-request support. Lab 2 implements Go-Back-N and Selective Repeat reliable data transfer protocols over UDP with sliding-window logic and binary packet serialization.

nodes.label: Lab1 String Utilities
nodes.description: Low-level string utilities for the proxy cache layer: byte array to lowercase hexadecimal conversion and SHA-256 hashing of strings. Used by the proxy handler to generate deterministic cache-key filenames from request URIs.

nodes.label: Lab1 File Utilities
nodes.description: Persistent cache storage for the HTTP proxy: serializes and deserializes arbitrary Java objects to/from disk using ObjectOutputStream/ObjectInputStream, enabling the proxy to store and recover cached HttpResponse objects between sessions.

nodes.label: Lab1 Site Blocking Filter
nodes.description: Check whether the requested hostname is present in the blocked-sites set.

nodes.label: Lab1 User Blocking Filter
nodes.description: Check whether the client IP is present in the blocked-users set.

nodes.label: Lab1 Redirect Filter
nodes.description: Resolve a host-specific redirect target from the redirect map when one exists.

nodes.label: Lab1 HTTP Header Parsing
nodes.description: Read raw bytes until the CRLF-CRLF header terminator and return the complete HTTP header string.

nodes.label: Lab1 HTTP Fixed-Length Body Parsing
nodes.description: Implement the fixed-length body helper that reads exactly the declared byte count or fails on early EOF.

nodes.label: Lab1 HTTP Line Reader
nodes.description: Implement the CRLF-delimited line reader used by chunked body parsing.

nodes.label: Lab1 HTTP Chunked Body Parsing
nodes.description: Implement the chunked-transfer decoder that reads hexadecimal chunk sizes, payload bytes, and the terminating zero chunk.

nodes.label: Lab1 HTTP Request Parsing
nodes.description: Parse an incoming client request line, header map, and optional fixed-length body into an HttpRequest.

nodes.label: Lab1 HTTP Response Parsing
nodes.description: Parse an upstream response status line, headers, and either fixed-length or chunked body bytes into an HttpResponse.

nodes.label: Lab1 HTTP Chunked Body Forwarding
nodes.description: Implement the private helper that writes response bytes using HTTP chunked transfer coding.

nodes.label: Lab1 HTTP Fixed-Length Body Forwarding
nodes.description: Implement the private helper that streams raw response body bytes for Content-Length transfers.

nodes.label: Lab1 HTTP Request Forwarding
nodes.description: Forward an HttpRequest by emitting its request line, headers, blank line, and any fixed-length body bytes.

nodes.label: Lab1 HTTP Response Forwarding
nodes.description: Forward an HttpResponse by emitting the status line, headers, and either fixed-length or chunked body encoding.

nodes.label: Lab1 HTTP Server Connection
nodes.description: Open a TCP socket to the upstream host and port using the provided timeout value.

nodes.label: Lab1 HTTP If-Modified-Since Helper
nodes.description: Return a request clone with the `If-Modified-Since` header derived from the cached response timestamp.

nodes.label: Lab1 Cache Manager
nodes.description: Disk-backed response cache for the proxy. Hashes request URIs into stable cache filenames, checks for cached serialized responses on disk, recovers hits through FileUtils, and persists fresh responses for later reuse.

nodes.label: Lab2 Packet & ACK Serialization
nodes.description: Binary wire-format serialization for both reliable-transfer protocols. Packet uses a fixed 1028-byte layout: 1-byte unsigned sequence number, 1024-byte payload, 2-byte unsigned data length, 1-byte EOF flag. ACK uses a 1-byte layout carrying a single unsigned sequence number. All GBN and SR code depends on these two classes.

nodes.label: Lab2 Transfer Support Utilities
nodes.description: Shared support utilities for the reliable-transfer labs. IOUtils loads input files into memory and appends received bytes to output files. Timer manages retransmission callbacks, supports cancellation, and recreates its scheduler so senders can restart a timer after stopping it.

nodes.label: Lab2 Go-Back-N Protocol
nodes.description: Go-Back-N sender and receiver over UDP: the sender manages a shared sliding-window timer, sends packets within the window boundary, processes cumulative ACKs to advance the window base, and retransmits all unacknowledged packets on timeout. The receiver accepts only in-order packets, writes them to file, and sends cumulative ACKs; out-of-order arrivals trigger re-ACK of the last in-order packet.

nodes.label: Lab2 Selective Repeat Protocol
nodes.description: Selective Repeat sender and receiver over UDP: the sender maintains independent per-packet timers and retransmits only individually timed-out packets; the window base advances when leading consecutive ACKs arrive. The receiver buffers out-of-order in-window packets, delivers data in order as gaps are filled, and re-ACKs previous-window packets to handle sender retransmissions. Independent of GBN.

edges.label: CacheManager hashes URIs into cache filenames via StrUtils.hashString
CacheManager persists and recovers HttpResponse objects via FileUtils
Request parsing reuses the raw HTTP header reader.
Request parsing uses the fixed-length body helper for Content-Length bodies.
Response parsing reuses the raw HTTP header reader.
Response parsing uses the fixed-length body helper when Content-Length is present.
Chunked body parsing depends on the CRLF line reader.
Response parsing uses the chunked body helper for Transfer-Encoding: chunked responses.
Request forwarding relies on the fixed-length body writer when a request body is present.
Response forwarding uses the fixed-length body writer for Content-Length responses.
Response forwarding uses the chunked body writer for chunked responses.
GBNSender calls Packet.toBytes and ACK.fromBytes; GBNReceiver calls Packet.fromBytes and ACK.toBytes
SRSender calls Packet.toBytes and ACK.fromBytes; SRReceiver calls Packet.fromBytes and ACK.toBytes
GBN sender/receiver load and append file bytes via IOUtils and schedule retransmissions via Timer
SR sender/receiver load and append file bytes via IOUtils and schedule retransmissions via Timer
